unmapped_risk: A sudden US Treasury designation of TSMC’s non-US advanced packaging and ABF substrate suppliers under secondary sanctions forces immediate compliance cutoffs. This bypasses normal rulemaking, triggers automatic inventory write-downs, and halts all non-American AI client orders overnight without regulatory notice.
underweighted_risk: CoWoS volume is structurally bottlenecked by <3 global suppliers for specialized ABF substrates and thermal interface materials. Markets price wafer fab capacity but systematically ignore that a port closure or export ban on these chemical/material inputs creates a 6-9 month production paralysis. The consensus treats packaging as infinitely scalable relative to silicon, masking a hard physical ceiling.
structural_fragility: TSMC’s >60% advanced node output is geographically locked to the Hsinchu-Tainan semiconductor corridor with zero operational redundancy. A single disruption to high-voltage transmission infrastructure or regional power grid stability instantly caps global AI hardware scaling, as no alternative fab on Earth can replicate N2/3nm CoWoS throughput within 18 months.
contagion_path: A CoWoS production halt forces NVDA and AVGO into immediate order cancellations and revenue recognition pauses. Hyperscalers freeze CapEx commitments, triggering SMH drawdown through semiconductor ETF rebalancing. QQQ suffers tech-weight collapse as AI infrastructure multiple compression accelerates. LITE’s photonics deployment roadmap is voided without active cluster builds, compounding sector-wide liquidity stress.
watch_items: US Treasury OFAC SDN list additions targeting Taiwanese semiconductor equipment or ABF substrate suppliers
Sudden spikes in Strait of Malacca chemical shipping insurance rates or abrupt delays in ASML EUV maintenance license renewals to TSMC
